text.title: Testing with pytest Failure Modes
text.summary: 5 antipatterns observed when testing-pytest is applied incorrectly. Each entry has a symptom (`description`), root cause (`reason`), and corrective fix (in `note`).

antipattern.description: Test passes alone, fails when others ran first.
antipattern.note: Fix: Demote to function scope or freeze the object with `dataclasses.replace`/`copy.deepcopy` on use.

antipattern.reason: Patch target is wrong (patched where defined, not where used).
antipattern.note: Fix: Always patch the import path in the module under test, not the origin module.

antipattern.description: CI is slow because every test starts the Django app.
antipattern.note: Fix: Move to `session` scope read-only fixture; use `django-pytest` if available.

antipattern.reason: Tests share a SQLite file or write to the same path.
antipattern.note: Fix: Use `tmp_path_factory` per worker, or `pytest-xdist` worker-id env var to namespace artefacts.

antipattern.description: Async test silently skipped, marked passed.
antipattern.reason: `pytest-asyncio` is in `auto` mode and the test forgot `@pytest.mark.asyncio`.
antipattern.note: Fix: Set `asyncio_mode = strict`; every async test must opt in.
